steps.title: Identify the clause structure and sentence type
steps.content: The blank must complete a relative clause that modifies iron gates. This sentence is a complete declarative statement about the sculpture's pattern.

steps.title: Apply the sentence punctuation and clause word order rules
steps.content:
Declarative sentences end with periods (not question marks), and relative clauses with omitted relative pronouns must use normal word order. “Soto would see in her neighborhood.” ends the sentence with a period and maintains normal word order in the relative clause.

Choices A and D violate the punctuation rule (using question marks instead of periods), and Choice C — would Soto see in her neighborhood. violates the word order rule (inverting instead of normal order).

That matches Choice B — Soto would see in her neighborhood..
